title: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Francistown Town Council (abattoir) Bye-laws
title_en: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Francistown Town Council (abattoir) Bye-laws

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Bye-laws may be cited as the Francistown Town Council (Abattoir) Bye-laws*.
*Originally made under the Township Act now repealed, these regulations have been continued under s 94(2) of the Local Government Act, 2013.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Tariff of charges
provisions.content:
3. Tariff of charges
(1) The following fees shall be paid to the council for services rendered or facilities provided at the abattoir
( a ) use of abattoir for slaughtering, lairage water, inspection and stamping of meat shall be as follows-

definitions.definition: the municipal abattoir, and includes the area set aside by the council for such purpose and demarcated as such, together with any building, space, pen, enclosure and lairage therein
and bull, ox, cow, heifer, steer, calf, sheep, lamb, goat, kid, pig or other quadruped used for human consumption
a veterinary surgeon approved by the Director of Veterinary Services
the person appointed by the council to perform the functions of manager of the abattoir or any person appointed by the council to act in his stead

the flesh and bone of any slaughtered animal whether in its natural state or subjected to any freezing, chilling or other preservative process, and also includes sausages, polonies, chopped or minced meat or any other meat similarly prepared
any person appointed by the council after consultation with the Director of Veterinary Services for the purposes of examining any slaughtered animal intended for human consumption
a medical practitioner appointed by the Director of Medical Services to act as medical officer in respect of Francistown
the stomach, intestines and other internal organs of any slaughtered animal not being red offal
the head, horns, feet, tail, heart, lungs, liver, kidneys and spleen of any slaughtered animal
a person appointed as such by the council.
